title: Expert-in-the-Loop AI for High-Stakes Financial Auditing
conference: Engineering a prioritization engine for terabyte-scale audit logs. Integrating domain expertise to augment, not replace, auditor judgment.

title: AI Feedback to Augment Reflection in Presentation Preparation
conference: Led full-stack implementation (Google Slides add-on) and a technology probe (N=13) with product managers using real-world slide decks.

title: Explainable AI for Customer Retention Strategy
conference: Built an explainable churn model (XGBoost/SHAP) and data pipeline (93.9% consistency gain) to empower service teams with actionable, data-driven insights.

title: Human-AI Alignment via Metacognitive Self-Revision
conference: Architected a self-revising agent that aligns with user intent. Deployed to 500+ users, validating robustness through structured feedback.

title: Calibrating Trust Under LLM Inconsistency
conference: Designed controlled experiment (N=252). Led statistical analysis in Python and R. Built full-stack experimental infrastructure.

title: Real-Time Background Segmentation
conference: Developed and deployed real-time segmentation model (PyTorch), achieving 1.5× speed and 34% accuracy improvement.

title: Sound Clouds: Participatory Music Generation
conference: Fine-tuned YOLO12n (98.3% accuracy). Conducted data collection, analysis, and visualization (N=35) on user interaction patterns.

title: VAL: Interactive Task Planning Interface
conference: Developed React-based interactive GUI with real-time WebSocket communication for HTN planning agent.

title: LTE Network KPI Analysis
conference: Analyzed KPI correlations on LTE eNB datasets using PCA to assess input-output predictability.

title: FORECST: Online Hackathon Platform
conference: Led full-stack development (React.js, Firebase) of an online platform hosting virtual hackathons, managing user registration, submission, and real-time interaction.

title: MYFIT: Clothing Size Recommendation App
conference: Built Android app in Java in a team of 3 for visualizing clothing sizes.

title: PINTOS: Operating System Implementation
conference: Implemented kernel threads, user programs, file system, and virtual memory in C on QEMU.
